name: Bacalhau
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the dried cod in cold water for about 24 hours, or until completely moistened. Change the water several times; drain thoroughly.
2. Put the cod into a saucepan with cold water to cover. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes or until fish is tender. Drain; skin and bone the fish. Flake with a fork into large pieces.
3. Saute the onions in half the butter until they are tender and golden.
4. Boil the unpeeled potatoes in salted water. Once tender, remove from the heat, refresh in running cold water, then remove skins. Drain and slice into 1/4-inch pieces.
5. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 1-1/2 quart casserole with the remaining butter.
6. Arrange a layer of half the potatoes, then half the cod, then half the onions on top. Sprinkle with a little pepper.
7. Repeat layering with more potato, cod, and onion.
8. Sprinkle bread crumbs over the top layer.
9. Bake for 15 minutes, or until heated through and lightly browned.
10. Before serving, garnish with olives and eggs, and sprinkle with parsley. Serve with wine vinegar and olive oil in cruets and pepper on the side.
